title: Temple Security Force

The '''Temple Security Force''' was the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security police]] of the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] and the [[Temple district/Legends|Temple Precinct]] surrounding it on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Though the name was traditionally and generally applied to the non-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and [[Police officer/Legends|police officers]], Temple Security also included the [[Jedi Peacekeeper]]s and the [[Jedi Temple Guard/Legends|Jedi Temple Guards]] that were stationed on Coruscant. When not researching crime and assisting other jurisdictions in solving cases, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Peacekeepers would also patrol and could specialize as [[Jedi Sniper|snipers]] and [[Jedi brute|brutes]]. The [[Jedi Gate Master]] would supervise groups of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] to guard the entrances of Temple during the day and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] from the Security Force to stand watch at night. At the time of the clone wars, it is known that the number of Security Forces was 500 soldiers.

The Temple Security Force, specifically those who were not [[The Force/Legends|Force]] sensitive, protected the Jedi Temple as well as providing police services for the citizens living in the Temple Precinct that surrounded the Temple. They fought side by side with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in defending the Temple during several conflicts including the [[Sacking of Coruscant]] during the [[Great Galactic War]] and the [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|invasion of the Temple]] at the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in which many lost their lives, including [[Cin Drallig]] and [[Jurokk]]. During the days of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], those who survived [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which instructed all Jedi and their associates to be killed, joined [[Whiplash]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and continued their duties by helping Jedi escape from the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. With the [[New Jedi Temple|rebuilding]] of the Jedi Temple under [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[New Jedi Order]], the Temple Security Force returned to their posts to protect the Jedi and their sanctuary from any threat. This included attacks by [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] soldiers such as the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] and [[Galactic Alliance Security]]; both from the very government the Jedi were to protect and defend.

The history of the Temple Security Force was first written down in ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'' by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Crix Sunburris]], a well respected [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]] and [[Jedi ace|ace]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]].

==Overview==
[[File:Jedi_Coruscant_Peacekeeper.jpg|thumb|left|200px|[[Jedi Sniper]]s stood guard atop the Temple]]
The Temple Security Force was a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security police force]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] tasked with protecting the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] and [[New Jedi Temple|its successor]], the Temple's [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] occupants and [[Jedi artifact/Legends|treasures]], and those civilians visiting or living in the surrounding [[Temple district/Legends|Temple Precinct]].<ref name="TJP" />

Temple Security was divided into two groups. The largest and most visible group was composed of non-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] beings. Trained as [[Police officer/Legends|police]] and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], their ranks included some former Jedi who had been unable to pass their [[Initiate Trials/Legends|Jedi Initiate Trials]] yet wanted to continue serving the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Though the name "Temple Security" was not always applied to them, the second group consisted of Jedi who had trained as [[Jedi Peacekeeper|Peacekeepers]], a specialization under the field of study of the [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]]. Many Peacekeepers assisted the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Security Force]] or studied and tracked criminal activity. Those Peacekeepers who patrolled the Temple itself could also specialize as [[Jedi Sniper|snipers]] and [[Jedi brute|brutes]]. The [[Jedi Gate Master]] was tasked with overseeing the security of the entrances to the Temple including the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Main Entrance]], the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Public Entrance]], and the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Reception Hall]]. Along with the regular security officers, the Gate Master would also supervise rotating groups of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] who guarded the Temple entrances by day and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] who stood guard at night.<ref name="TJP" />

==History==
Throughout the Temple, agents were deployed to safeguard various chambers. Two officers were regularly posted at the [[Jedi Temple central security station/Legends|central security station]] where they monitored the security cameras running throughout the complex. During the [[Sacking of Coruscant]] of [[3653 BBY]], the Temple Security Forces attempted to help the Jedi defend the Temple from an assault of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and [[Imperial soldier]]s led by [[Darth Malgus]], though they seemed to be no match for Force-sensitive opponents.<ref name="SWTOR" /> Agents were deployed to the bowels of the Temple in [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]] when Master [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] discovered the murderous [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Xanatos]] lurking in the water tunnels. While Xanatos escaped, Security recovered his mode of transportation in to the waterway and confiscated it.<ref name="TCT">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Captive Temple]]''</ref> Several years later, at the height of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] the Security Force was under the command of Chief of Security [[Battlemaster/Legends|Temple battlemaster]] [[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]]. At the end of the war, the Republic's duplicitous [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] issued [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] ordering the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] to invade the Temple and kill its occupants. Remaining loyal to the Jedi, the non-Force-users and Jedi alike fought against the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] during [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]] until their numbers almost completely diminished. Those that escaped with their lives joined the underground group known as [[Whiplash]].<ref name="JT" />

Following the reestablishment of the [[New Jedi Order|Order]], [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] decided to reinstate the tradition of a security force at the Temple and began to build up a civilian group. While it is unknown if Gate Masters were appointed in the New Order, officers of the Order were once more tested and proved loyal following assaults by both [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Natasi Daala]]. During the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], the security forces retreated with the Order to the [[Jedi Outpost]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to aid in the protection of the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiates]] there.<ref name="REV" /> Years later in [[43 ABY]] the security of the Temple was still active as new security measures were put in place to protect against invasion. During the incident at the Temple caused by [[Jysella Horn]]'s [[Force psychosis]], Security was called to the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] and the main entrance to capture her. However, using a form of [[flow-walking]], Horn escaped.<ref name="Omen">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Omen]]''</ref>

{{Military-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Temple_Security_Force.jpg|thumb|right|120px|Temple Security from ''Star Wars: Battlefront II.]]
In ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', the Temple Security Forces appear in the [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|''Knighfall'']] mission of the [[Rise of the Empire era|Rise of the Empire]] campaign. In the game, the Temple Soldiers wore uniforms closely resembling those worn by soldiers of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]], except in all grey and with slightly different hats. Like the Naboo soldiers seen in the [[Battle of Naboo (Imperial Period)|next mission]] and in the [[Star Wars: Battlefront|previous game]], they are equipped with blaster rifles which bear a striking resemblance to the [[DH-17 blaster rifle/Legends|DH-17 blaster rifle]] that fires green bolts.

In ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', the Temple Security Force were initially suggested to be members of the [[Republic Special Forces Division]], but this has proven to be false. While the Republic Special Forces weren't established until after the [[Treaty of Coruscant]], the Temple Security Force were shown to exist (at the [[Sacking of Coruscant]]) before the treaty was ever signed.
